title: PM Modi wants Indians to pause gold buying: How much forex can be saved?
content: India’s gold imports hit a record $71.98 billion in 2025-26, rising over 24% from $58 billion a year earlier. This was largely due to skyrocketing global gold prices, which have bumped up the value of imported gold.

title: 'I don't trust Pakistan': Top US lawmaker demands another mediator in Iran war
content: Senator Lindsey Graham has questioned Pakistan's credibility as a mediator in US-Iran negotiations, citing reports of Iranian military aircraft using Pakistani bases. Pakistan denies these claims, stating aircraft were for diplomatic arrangements. Broader scrutiny of Pakistan's mediation role has emerged within the Trump administration due to stalled talks and Iran's response to peace proposals.

title: Trump's trade war kills Canadian tourism to US, data shows 42% drop
content: Canadian tourism to the US has plummeted by an average of 42%, significantly impacting major cities and industry-linked mid-sized towns. This sharp decline, attributed to President Trump's trade war, also affects business travel, with fewer tech and finance employees journeying for work. The future of cross-border travel recovery remains uncertain under current trade conditions.

title: Sai Sudharsan equals Virat Kohli's massive IPL record
content: Sai Sudharsan's composed 61 off 44 balls guided Gujarat Titans to 168/5 against Sunrisers Hyderabad. His innings marked his third consecutive IPL season with over 500 runs, a feat previously achieved by only a few elite players. Sudharsan continues to be a standout performer with an impressive strike rate.

title: ‘Removed uterus left in box’: 4 women die after C-section surgery in Kota
content: Another woman has died from post-cesarean section complications at Kota's New Medical College Hospital, marking the fourth fatality in a week. Pinki Mahawar, 30, succumbed after being transferred from JK Lon Hospital, with her husband alleging a delayed referral. The incident has led to protests and calls for accountability regarding safety protocols in government hospitals.

title: CSK likely to rope in UAE-raised Karnataka batter Noronha
content: Chennai Super Kings are reportedly set to sign Karnataka's Macneil Hadley Noronha as an injury replacement for Ramakrishna Ghosh for the remainder of IPL 2026. Noronha, who had a stellar C.K. Nayudu Trophy campaign, impressed during trials with multiple franchises. This move comes as CSK faces a season heavily disrupted by injuries to key players.

title: A US family sold their company for $1.7 bn and gave 540 workers $240 mn of it
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: Yogi of northeast? How 'outsider' Himanta emerged as BJP's next-gen leader
content: After winning the BJP a brute majority in the 2026 Assam assembly polls, the question now is whether Himanta Biswa Sarma stands at par with chief ministers like Yogi Adityanath and Devendra Fadnavis, who are widely regarded as disciplined Sangh loyalists and potential next generation leaders of the BJP in the post Modi-Shah era.

title: Why Vijay's floor test in Tamil Nadu is really a survival test for AIADMK
content: Tamil Nadu's trust vote for CM-designate Vijay is overshadowed by a potential AIADMK rupture. Senior rebels are questioning party chief Palaniswami's leadership, accusing him of seeking an understanding with the rival DMK. This internal strife could signal the AIADMK's terminal decline as its MLAs consider shifting loyalty.

title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs and CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making priorities clear for all employees
content: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: Penguins eye $7.8M Stuart Skinner trade to Senators in latest NHL shake-up
content: Stuart Skinner has been a potential free agency target for the Ottawa Senators since his days with the Edmonton Oilers, and now he’s being linked to the Pittsburgh Penguins. Ottawa could be a player because of defensive inconsistencies and poor penalty kill rankings, NHL insiders say. His $7.8 million contract situation and Pittsburgh’s goalie competition may open the door for a major offseason move.

title: NHL rumor links Matthew Knies with Blackhawks, Canadiens, Utah Mammoth
content: Matthew Knies has become one of the hottest names in the latest NHL trade rumors after reports connected the Toronto Maple Leafs winger to a handful of interested teams. After another underwhelming season across the National Hockey League, Toronto is looking at possible roster changes in the offseason, with the Chicago Blackhawks, Montreal Canadiens and Utah Mammoth reportedly watching closely.

title: Dianna Russini and husband stay public amid Mike Vrabel controversy
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and the former New York Times’ popular NFL insider, Dianna Russini, have remained distant from each other ever since their affair rumors sparked outrage among millions around the world. As Dianna Russini stays at home after resigning from her position at The Athletic, all eyes have been on her husband, Kevin Goldschmidt.

title: Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano: Date, time and Netflix streaming details
content: MMA legend Ronda Rousey is finally set to make her long-awaited return to combat sports this Saturday in a blockbuster fight against Gina Carano. The highly anticipated clash will headline the first-ever live MMA event streamed on Netflix. Fans have waited years to see the two pioneers of women’s MMA face each other after talks of a fight first surfaced more than a decade ago.

title: Petrol & diesel prices frozen for 4 years - how deep is India's fuel dilemma?
content: India assures stable fuel supplies despite global disruptions and rising crude prices. However, unchanged domestic rates are causing significant losses for oil marketing companies, leading to a pressing question about potential price hikes. The government faces a fiscal burden from absorbing price shocks through tax cuts.

title: Reports claim Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar have parted ways
content: Rumours surrounding the marriage of Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar have intensified after reports claimed that the couple has allegedly divorced after four years of marriage. Reports further suggest that cheating may have been one of the reasons behind their reported separation.

title: LeBron James admits uncertainty over retirement after Lakers playoff exit
content: LeBron James admitted he is unsure about continuing his NBA career after the Lakers were swept by the Oklahoma City Thunder. The 41-year-old said he will discuss his future with family before making a decision. James added that staying committed to the daily basketball process will play a major role. The Lakers star is entering free agency this offseason after another strong season.

title: Will Varun Chakravarthy play vs RCB? Massive update on KKR mystery spinner
content: Kolkata Knight Riders face a must-win IPL 2026 clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru, with star spinner Varun Chakravarthy's fitness a major concern. Assistant coach Shane Watson expressed optimism about the team's recent form and potential playoff push, highlighting Angkrish Raghuvanshi's impressive skill development.

title: Rs 16.77L cr gone! Market crash over 4 days leaves investors with deep losses; what's next?
content: Persistent foreign fund outflows and the rupee sliding to record lows have further dampened sentiment, prompting investors to stay cautious and intensify selling across equity markets.

title: R Madhavan shares parenting lesson he learnt while raising son Vedaant
content: R Madhavan emphasizes instilling gratitude and awareness of privilege in his son Vedaant, contrasting extravagant modern parenting with grounded values. He advocates for filling children's time with passionate activities until adolescence to prevent dwelling on negative influences, a lesson learned from a Canadian family.

title: NEET UG 2026 leak: Rahul asks students to ‘Google’ ex-NTA DG’s posting; attacks BJP
content: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi intensified his attack on the Centre after the cancellation of NEET-UG 2026, asking students to “Google” the current posting of the former NTA Director General linked to the NEET-UG 2024 controversy. In a strongly worded X post, Gandhi alleged that the BJP government protects and promotes officials associated with examination irregularities. His remarks came hours after the NTA cancelled NEET-UG 2026 and ordered a fresh examination amid allegations of paper leaks and widespread irregularities.

title: 'Over 100 matching questions': Rajasthan 'guess paper' clue blows open NEET-UG 2026 scandal
content: The National Testing Agency has cancelled the NEET-UG 2026 examination. This decision follows evidence of a significant overlap between circulated guess papers and the actual question paper. The Central Bureau of Investigation will now conduct a comprehensive probe into the alleged paper leak and malpractice network. Over 24 lakh aspirants are affected by this unprecedented move.

title: Why Gujarat Titans changed jersey colour vs Sunrisers Hyderabad
content: Pat Cummins won the toss and chose to field as Sunrisers Hyderabad faced Gujarat Titans. The Titans sported a special lavender jersey to support cancer awareness. Both teams, with identical points, retained their playing XIs for the crucial IPL 2026 encounter. Captains Cummins and Gill expressed confidence in their strategies.

title: Israel sent Iron Dome system to UAE to defend against attacks by Iran
content: Israel has reportedly deployed its Iron Dome air defence system to protect the UAE, a significant development following recent Iranian drone and missile attacks on the Gulf nation. This cooperation, confirmed by US Ambassador Mike Huckabee, highlights deepening ties between the two Abraham Accord signatories. The UAE has faced extensive aerial assaults, prompting this strategic military assistance.

title: Kaante dispute: Pritish Nandy Communications gets Rs 2.55 crore settlement from Bombay HC
content: The Bombay High Court has officially disposed of the commercial appeal regarding the 2002 film 'Kaante', following a settlement between Pritish Nandy Communications and White Feather Films. As per the consent terms approved on April 21, 2026, Sanjay Gupta’s production house will pay a lump sum of Rs. 2.55 crore to PNC within 60 days. This settlement resolves a 2016 arbitration dispute that originally involved a claim of Rs. 3.52 crore. Failure to pay within the stipulated window will result in the resumption of original litigation proceedings.

title: 822/4 and a 794-run win! Cricket match produces unreal scorecard
content: In a Zimbabwe domestic cricket match, Scorpions Cricket Club achieved an astonishing 822/4, powered by centuries from Winfed Mutende (203 off 75 balls) and Takunda Madembo (302). Methane Lions collapsed to 28/7, resulting in a record 794-run victory for Scorpions. This score dwarfs previous high totals in List A cricket.

title: ‘A mature captain never ... ': Ex-India cricketer questions Axar Patel’s leadership
content: Mohammad Kaif criticized Axar Patel for publicly singling out teammate Kuldeep Yadav during Delhi Capitals' challenging IPL 2026 season. Kaif emphasized that experienced captains avoid public criticism, especially of proven performers like Kuldeep, advocating for team-focused accountability instead.  Despite this, Axar played a crucial captain's knock in a recent win.

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay meets AIADMK rebel leaders ahead of crucial floor test
content: Tamil Nadu faces an AIADMK internal conflict as Vijay's new government prepares for a floor test. A faction led by CV Shanmugam pledged support to Vijay, defying party directives. Agri SS Krishnamurthy warned rebel MLAs of action, while OS Manian asserted 47 MLAs back Edappadi K Palaniswami. This rift highlights deep divisions within the AIADMK following recent poll performance.

title: Mouni Roy's husband Suraj Nambiar deletes his Instagram account amid divorce rumours
content: Speculation about Mouni Roy and Suraj Nambiar's marriage intensified as they reportedly unfollowed each other on Instagram, with Suraj's account now appearing deactivated. Adding to the buzz, Mouni's friend Disha Patani also allegedly unfollowed Suraj. Fans expressed concern over the alleged separation, recalling the couple's 2022 wedding.

title: The superpower trap: Who has more cards - Trump or Xi?
content: President Trump's China visit occurs amid a US-Iran stalemate, with analysts suggesting Xi Jinping holds more leverage. Many in Beijing view Trump as a symptom of liberal democracy's instability and American decline, validating Xi's "rise of the East" worldview. This perception of US weakness could lead China to miscalculate and test Washington.

title: India’s retail inflation jumps to over one-year high at 3.48 per cent in April
content: India's retail inflation climbed to a 13-month high of 3.48% in April, primarily fueled by soaring food prices. Consumer Food Price Index accelerated to 4.20%, with rural inflation outpacing urban rates. While silver and gold jewellery saw significant price hikes, tomatoes surged, but potatoes and onions experienced deflation. This marks the second consecutive monthly rise, nearing the RBI's target.

title: 'Those eyes don't lie': Krunal Pandya on Virat Kohli
content: Krunal Pandya continues to be a vital all-rounder for RCB in their IPL 2026 title defence, recently scoring a match-winning 73 despite severe cramps against Mumbai Indians. He also achieved his 100th IPL wicket and draws inspiration from Virat Kohli's unwavering intensity and dedication, having previously won Player of the Match in two IPL finals.

title: 'Mere liye raita phaila kar gayi hain': Brij Bhushan on Vinesh Phogat controversy
content: Former WFI president Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh stated that Vinesh Phogat's participation in wrestling trials is under the current WFI administration's purview. He defended his tenure, asserting the WFI never barred athletes without valid reasons. Phogat was declared ineligible by the WFI until 2026 due to ongoing disciplinary proceedings, a decision she disputes, citing international clearance.

title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled: How a 'guess paper' leak derailed the exam and what happens next
content: NEET-UG 2026 exam has been cancelled by the National Testing Agency due to a massive "guess paper" leak, impacting over 22 lakh students. The government has ordered a CBI probe into the alleged irregularities. The re-examination dates will be announced soon, with existing registrations carrying forward. This decision aims to protect the integrity of the national examination system.

title: Dianna Russini’s public moment sparks “staged PR” claims online
content: Dianna Russini and her husband Kevin Goldschmidt sparked intense online speculation after photos of the couple kissing outside their New Jersey home went viral. The public display of affection came amid ongoing rumours linking Russini to Patriots coach Mike Vrabel, leading many social media users to question whether the moment was staged. Some even floated theories about an alleged open relationship, though no evidence supports those claims. The controversy surrounding Russini and Vrabel continues dominating online conversations.

title: BJP MLA Tapas Roy sworn in as Pro-tem Speaker of West Bengal Assembly
content: Tapas Roy is among the seniormost MLAs elected to the 18th West Bengal Legislative Assembly; according to BJP sources, he may be nominated for the post of Speaker

title: Four cheetah cubs die at Kuno in suspected leopard attack
content: The deaths in Madhya Pradesh hold significance as the four cubs were the first to be born in the open forest since India’s reintroduction programme for the big cat species — Project Cheetah — was launched in 2022; this is the largest number of cheetah casualties at Kuno in one instance, which witnessed the birth of several cubs in recent months.

title: All India strike by MGNREGA workers on May 15
content: NREGA Sangharsh Morcha and All India Kisan Sabha have called for the strike against the government’s decision to discontinue the scheme.

title: IMD unveils weather model to provide ‘block level’ forecast of monsoon journey
content: It has been a long-standing aim of the IMD to provide hyper local forecasts to enable farmers to time their sowing precisely

title: Minister urges Andhra nurses to earn Kerala-like recognition in Guntur
content: Addressing the International Nurses Day celebrations at Government Medical College, Satya Kumar Yadav says nurses provide emotional support and comfort to patients through compassionate service and care

title: Tamil Nadu government appoints Rickey Pandit Vettrivel as OSD (Political) to Chief Minister
content: CPI(M) state secretary P. Shanmugam said that it was unacceptable to appoint an astrologer as the Officer-on-Duty (Politics) to the Chief Minister

title: NTA cancels NEET-UG 2026, to conduct re-test after alleged paper leak
content: NTA to announce re-test schedule in seven to 10 days; no new registration, fees; “any move short of cancelling exams would signal that those trying to scam the system would have succeeded,” says NTA DG

title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled LIVE: CBI takes custody of man detained by Nashik Police
content: The schedule for the re-examination would be announced within the “next seven to 10 days”, according to the DG of NTA, Abhishek Singh

title: Intellectuals’ forum seeks clarity on river water sharing talks
content: Alochanaparula Vedika raises concern over proposals discussed by Chief Minister with Union Jal Shakti Minister; opposes Palamuru-Rangareddy project and upstream diversion of Godavari waters

title: PM-led key panel meets to decide on next CBI Director
content: The key committee — which includes PM Modi and LoP Rahul Gandhi is responsible for finalising the selection of next CBI Director. The selection is done from a panel of officers.

title: Opposition accuses Modi government of jeopardising students’ future over recurrent NEET paper leaks
content: Opposition parties demand accountability from the National Testing Agency (NTA), which conducts the examination; Rahul Gandhi accuses Prime Minister Narendra Modi and BJP of being “partners in the theft” of students’ future.

title: Left parties slam NEET paper leak, demand scrapping of NTA
content: CPI(M), CPI call for decentralised exam system, probe into irregularities, and accountability for lapses; repeated leaks, exam scams, cancellations point to a “collapse” of the exam system, says CPI leader D. Raja

title: Bigger the theft, the bigger the reward, says Rahul Gandhi in a dig at bureaucratic appointments in Bengal
content: Congress general secretary (communications) Jairam Ramesh claims the appointments show ‘brazen collusion and connivance’ between the EC and the BJP

title: NEET exam cancellation: Students say they are uncertain and frustrated
content: Karnataka ministers term it a ‘grave injustice to lakhs of aspiring medical students’ and sought power for States to conduct medical entrance tests

title: NEET-UG 2026 cancellation triggers outrage, anxiety among aspirants in A.P.
content: Parents fear that the delay and uncertainty may affect students’ confidence and mental well-being; similar incidents reported from Gujarat, Bihar, and Rajasthan point to a growing paper leak network, says APCC president

title: RSS’s Hosabale hits out at Udhayanidhi Stalin over Sanatan remarks, bats for dialogue with Pakistan
content: Hosabale described Sanatan Dharma as an enduring civilisational value that cannot be erased by political criticism

title: Ghosts of the darkroom: the custodians of Thiruvananthapuram’s collective memory
content: The city’s veteran lensmen reflect on a craft that has moved from a rare miracle to a digital heartbeat

title: Gold imports surge in India: An explainer
content: Gold imports in India are rising sharply, raising concerns about the economy and trade deficit. What’s driving this surge and why it matters now, here’s a quick explainer.

title: Gauhati HC strikes down NC Hills Autonomous Council's anti-defection law
content: The HC held that the NC Hills Autonomous Council, a body under the Sixth Schedule of the Constitution, is not a plenary legislature and lacks competence beyond enumerated subjects to frame laws on

title: Watch: NEET UG 2026 cancelled, Govt orders CBI probe | Above the Fold | 12.05.2026
content: From the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak row and the AIADMK split in Tamil Nadu to Rahul Gandhi’s attack over West Bengal appointments, here are the top stories.

title: Thiruvananthapuram Fine Arts College celebrates legacy of T.K. Padmini
content: College Principal Manoj Kannan, who delivered the presidential address, said Padmini was far ahead of her time in integrating feminist perspectives and elements of German Expressionism into her art

title: Kumaraswamy announces fresh funds to set up 1,243 EV chargers in Karnataka
content: To date, proposals worth ₹503.86 crore have been approved, to facilitate the installation of 4,874 EV chargers in various States through Central Public Sector Enterprises (CPSEs) such as HPCL, IOCL, and BPCL

title: Tamil Nadu politics LIVE: AIADMK reacts sharply to rebel MLAs, AMMK expels party's lone legislator
content: Ahead of the crucial floor test where TVK-led government is set to prove its majority in the Legislative Assembly and amid a split in AIADMK, AMMK's lone MLA S. Kamaraj expelled from party for announcing his support to TVK.

title: Assembly polls 2026: Cautious strategy, poll wins reinforce IUML’s edge in Samastha ties
content: Several Samastha leaders are now more openly recognising IUML’s key role in the political landscape and its likely importance in forming and running the upcoming government

title: NTA complacency led to NEET cancellation: Fraternity Movement
content: It said the conduct of the examination, preparation of question paper and the alleged leak should be investigated thoroughly

title: MP highlights nurses’ role in patient care
content: Kesineni Sivanath pays floral tributes to the statue of Florence Nightingale on the occasion of International Nurses Day

title: CMDA pushes for faster clearances, issues strict instructions to prevent malpractice
content: A recently issued circular says all applications for planning permission, completion certificates, layouts, and reclassification must be completed at the earliest without any delay

title: ‘Peons ours, officers from outside’: Surjewala targets Haryana govt. over ‘brazen daylight robbery’ of jobs
content: Attacking the Haryana Public Service Commission and the Haryana Staff Selection Commission, Congress leader claims that 70-80% of officer posts are going to non-Haryanvis

title: PG resident doctors at Thiruvananthapuram MCH go on strike over non-payment of stipend
content: Outpatient clinics, inpatient services and elective operations at the hospital were partially affected

title: China’s Foreign Minister to skip BRICS Delhi meet over ‘scheduling reasons’
content: Beijing states that Chinese Ambassador to India Xu Feihong will be present; it expresses readiness to work with all BRICS members to support India as the Chair

title: Experts flag need to review and structurally reform the NTA
content: Following the cancellation of the NEET-UG 2026 paper, the National Testing Agency faces allegations of lax operational capacity, porous cybersecurity, poor crisis communication cancellation again

title: Coaching Centres, Scanners, Shadow Server: Intricate Web Of NEET Leak
content: NEET Leak: The first digital copy of the leaked medical entrance exam's question paper was prepared in Nashik, sources said.

title: Video Shows Putin Out And About In Moscow Amid 'Bunker' Reports
content: Putin's video, which was released late on Monday, appeared to be a visual rebuttal of those accusations and of assertions.

title: What Happens If UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer Quits, Or Is Challenged?
content: Some senior cabinet ministers, according to media reports, have told Starmer to announce a plan for his exit.

title: Pay Rs 2,000 To Live Like A Prisoner At Hyderabad Jail
content: Chanchalguda Central Prison in Hyderabad on Tuesday launched "Feel the Jail," an initiative that allows ordinary citizens to experience prison life for 12 or 24 hours.

title: Yamuna Still Toxic Despite Above-Normal Rainfall In Delhi: Report
content: Fresh data released by the Delhi Pollution Control Committee (DPCC) shows that large stretches of the river flowing through the capital remain severely polluted.

title: Assam Chief Minister Oath Ceremony Highlights: Himanta Sarma, 4 Ministers Sworn In
content: Assam Oath Ceremony LIVE Updates: Along with Himanta Biswa Sarma, four ministers - two from the BJP and one each from its allies Asom Gana Parishad (AGP) and Bodoland People's Front (BPF) - will also be sworn in.

title: Who Is Rickey Pandit, Astrologer Who Predicted TVK Win, Now Vijay's Adviser
content: He claims he predicted Jayalalithaa's victory in the 1991 elections and also warned her about a difficult phase between 1994 and 1997.

title: "Bing, Bing, Gone": Trump Shares AI Images Of Laser Attack On Iranian Aircraft
content: One image showed a US warship blasting an aircraft marked with the Iranian flag using what appeared to be a high-powered laser weapon.

title: Youth Unemployment Rises In Early 2026, Young Women Affected Most
content: India's youth faced high unemployment levels during January and March 2026. While the unemployment levels among the 15-29 age group remained elevated throughout the year, they rose in the final months of the financial year 2025-26.

title: 'Deal Firmly With Pak, But Keep Talks Window Open': Top RSS Leader
content: RSS General Secretary Dattatreya Hosabale recalled how during the time of Atal Bihari Vajpayee, the former prime minister kept a path open for dialogue, and even travelled to Lahore by bus

title: Gujarat Governor Orders Leaner Convoys, No Chopper Amid Fuel-Saving Push
content: Despite holding Z+ security status, the Governor has instructed that his convoy be reduced to the minimum number of vehicles required, significantly cutting fuel expenditure.

title: Middle East Crisis In Focus As BRICS Foreign Ministers Meet In India
content: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi will skip the meeting as his presence is required in Beijing for US President Donald Trump's three-day state visit, which coincides with the BRICS gathering.

title: Gujarat Prepares For Census 2027 With Digital Self-Enumeration Initiative
content: For the first time in the history of the Indian Census, citizens will be able to provide their household details through a dedicated online portal.

title: Union Minister Bandi Sanjay's Son Seeks Bail, Calls POCSO Case 'Honey-Trap'
content: The plea was moved before the vacation bench of the Telangana High Court and is scheduled to be heard on May 14.

title: Infiltration Attempt Foiled Along LoC In J&K's Poonch, Intruder Killed: Army
content: "Acting on persistent surveillance, suspicious movement was detected approximately 300 metres inside own territory in the general area of Krishna Ghati Sector, Poonch, along the LoC at around 4 pm today," the White Knight Corps said on X.

title: Tehran, Taiwan, Trade: Trump Heads To China With Xi Holding The Upper Hand
content: Despite years of harsh rhetoric on China, Donald Trump has continued to publicly praise Chinese President Xi Jinping. Just last week, he described Xi as a "good man" and a "smart man" with whom he has "a very good relationship".

title: Delhi Maximum Temperature Settles At 39 Degrees, Air Quality Remains Moderate
content: According to IMD data, Safdarjung, the city's primary weather station, recorded a maximum temperature of 39 degrees Celsius (0.5 degrees below normal), and no rainfall was recorded there until 5:30 pm.

Her work brings together reportage and analysis, tracking developments in India and abroad while examining the broader social and economic currents shaping public discourse. Read More

title: Vijay's floor test splits AIADMK. Will Thalapathy go the Yediyurappa way?
content: Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More
